message:
feat(fcp3): add semantic boundary preservation E2E tests and wire new FCP3 crates

Wire fcp-kernel, fcp-policy, and fcp-evidence into the workspace and
add E2E tests that enforce the FCP3 crate-carving boundaries.

Cargo.toml:
- Add fcp-kernel, fcp-policy, and fcp-evidence to workspace members
  and [workspace.dependencies] section. These are the three owner crates
  defined in docs/FCP3_Canonical_Owner_Map.md that will absorb the
  semantic responsibilities currently concentrated in fcp-core.

fcp-e2e/Cargo.toml:
- Add fcp-kernel, fcp-policy, fcp-evidence as dev-dependencies so
  boundary tests can verify import relationships across crate boundaries.

semantic_boundary_preservation_e2e.rs (151 lines, new):
- FCP3 regression tests verifying that types don't leak across semantic
  domain boundaries. Each test ensures a specific owner crate exposes
  the types its domain needs without consumers bypassing the intended
  owner by importing directly from fcp-core. This catches regressions
  where a refactoring accidentally re-exports types through the wrong
  crate, breaking the ownership model before it is fully enforced.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

  "wiremock",
 ]

+ "fcp-core",
+ "serde",
+ "serde_json",
+]
+
 [[package]]
 name = "fcp-exa"
 version = "0.1.0"
@@ -4550,6 +4560,16 @@ dependencies = [
  "wiremock",
 ]
 
+[[package]]
+name = "fcp-policy"
+version = "0.1.0"
+dependencies = [
+ "fcp-async-core",
+ "fcp-core",
+ "serde",
+ "serde_json",
+]

 [[package]]
 name = "fcp-postgresql"
 version = "0.1.0"
